title: Roasted Aardvark Peruvian Noodles Seco de Cordero
description: A hearty, buttery, and cozy Peruvian dish featuring noodles, cure, and a touch of sweetness. This recipe is a twist on the classic Seco de Cordero, made diabetic-friendly by using reduced sugar and a focus on protein-rich ingredients.

ingredients.name: aardvark fillets
ingredients.notes: Wild-caught, sustainably sourced aardvarks. Remove bloodlines and skin before use.

ingredients.name: Peruvian noodles
ingredients.notes: Thin, air-dried noodles. Look for a brand that is low in sodium.

ingredients.name: ground beef
ingredients.notes: 80/20 lean to fat ratio. Brown meat in small batches for best results.

ingredients.name: cured goat cheese
ingredients.notes: Aged for at least 6 months. Crumbly texture preferred.

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: Thinly slice onion and caramelize for added depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince garlic and saut1 until fragrant.

ingredients.name: chili peppers
ingredients.notes: Diced, seeded, and deveined. Use any variety of pepper for added heat.

ingredients.name: cumin
ingredients.notes: Ground cumin adds a warm, earthy flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika adds a smoky depth to the sauce.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Use a high-protein flour for best results.

ingredients.name: unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: Melted butter adds a rich, creamy texture to the noodles.

ingredients.name: chicken broth
ingredients.notes: Low-sodium broth preferred. Use store-bought or homemade for best flavor.

instructions: Season aardvark fillets with salt, pepper, and cumin. Roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until cooked through.
While aardvark is roasting, cook Peruvian noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, saut1 onion and garlic until fragrant. Add diced chili peppers and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add ground beef to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks. Cook until browned, then stir in cumin and paprika.
In a small bowl, whisk together flour and chicken broth. Add to the skillet, stirring to combine. Bring sauce to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, or until thickened.
Add cooked noodles to the skillet, tossing to combine with sauce. Stir in melted butter and crumbled cured goat cheese.
Return roasted aardvark to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon to combine with noodles and sauce.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
Let dish rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld together.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
